headings: Pick the weekend everyone can actually make.

guardrailFacts.corePathDefinition: npm run check failed, or at least two core acceptance items failed at both viewports.

findings.title: Destination vote buttons have indistinguishable accessible names
findings.evidence: Every unselected destination renders the same button text, "Vote for this", without a destination-specific aria-label. The deterministic control inventory consequently reports three separate enabled buttons with the identical name "Vote for this".
findings.impact: Screen-reader users navigating by controls cannot determine which destination each vote button selects without repeatedly exploring surrounding content.
findings.remediation: Include the destination name in each button's visible or accessible name, such as `aria-label={`Vote for ${destination.name}`}`.

findings.title: Sort control suppresses its focus indicator
findings.evidence: The destination sort select applies `outline-none`, while neither the select nor its containing label defines a `focus-visible` or `focus-within` replacement.
findings.impact: Keyboard users can lose track of focus when they reach this secondary but useful comparison control.
findings.remediation: Remove `outline-none` or add a clearly visible focus ring on the select or its `focus-within` container.

findings.title: Small metric labels fall below normal-text contrast requirements
findings.evidence: The reusable CardMetric component renders 12px labels in `#7a756d` on `#f6f1ea`; those colors have approximately 4.07:1 contrast, below the 4.5:1 threshold for normal text. This component labels every card's budget, travel mode, and vote count.
findings.impact: Users with low vision may have difficulty identifying the meaning of otherwise visible comparison values.
findings.remediation: Darken the label color or lighten the background until the combination reaches at least 4.5:1 contrast.

findings.title: Above-the-fold hero downloads an oversized PNG at every viewport
findings.evidence: The hero immediately loads `weekender-hero.png` through a single `src` with no responsive variants. Deterministic build evidence identifies that 1672×941 raster as a 2,490.11 kB production asset, accounting for most of the reported 2,771,498 production asset bytes even on the 390px viewport.
findings.impact: Mobile visitors must transfer and decode substantially more image data than their display needs, delaying initial rendering on constrained networks and devices.
findings.remediation: Encode the image in a modern format and provide appropriately sized responsive variants with `srcset` and `sizes`.

categories.label: Brief fidelity and functional correctness
evidence: The deterministic suite reports all nine desktop and all nine mobile acceptance checks passing, including destination comparison, budgets, travel times, vote state changes, no horizontal overflow, and no browser errors.

Four realistic destinations include per-person budgets, travel modes and times, existing votes, and three-day itineraries in source/src/App.tsx:54-207; source/src/App.tsx:393-418 renders the comparison cards, detail panels, group status, and winning itinerary.

The winner-itinerary heuristic mentions 0 detected itinerary regions, but source/src/App.tsx:765-807 unconditionally renders a visible "Winning itinerary" section with Friday, Saturday, and Sunday articles. This is a harness-region-detection limitation rather than a product defect.

categories.label: Reliability and state integrity
evidence: Vote totals and the winner are derived from immutable sample votes plus the single `userVote` identifier in source/src/App.tsx:231-259, so changing or retracting a vote does not increment mutable counters or allow multiple personal votes.
Winner ties use a deterministic match-score fallback in source/src/App.tsx:257-259, and destination rendering uses stable destination IDs as keys in source/src/App.tsx:393-403.
The deterministic interaction path completed at both viewports with zero console errors and zero page errors.

categories.label: Accessibility
evidence: The app uses native buttons and a native select, a main landmark, semantic headings, and descriptive hero alternative text in source/src/App.tsx:270-301 and source/src/App.tsx:357-405.
Deterministic checks found no enabled control lacking a detectable name and confirmed keyboard focus reached nine unique controls at each viewport.
Source review identified bounded barriers in repeated vote-button naming, sort-control focus visibility, and small metric-label contrast.

categories.label: Maintainability and code clarity
evidence: The data model is explicitly typed in source/src/App.tsx:26-50, filter and sort IDs are constrained unions in source/src/App.tsx:209-217, and strict TypeScript is enabled in source/tsconfig.json:2-17.
UI responsibilities are separated into focused components for vote rows, destination cards, details, budgets, group status, and itineraries in source/src/App.tsx:425-807.
The deterministic typecheck and production build both passed.

categories.label: Security, privacy, and dependency compliance
evidence: The dependency diff passed with no added, removed, or changed packages, and deterministic inspection found no remote references or external browser requests.
The source contains no network calls, credential collection, external links, or unsafe HTML injection; all rendered content comes from local typed sample data.
The application fulfills the no-sign-in/no-backend path at both tested viewports.

categories.label: Performance and responsive robustness
evidence: Both deterministic viewport checks report 0px document-level horizontal overflow, and responsive grid transitions are defined throughout source/src/App.tsx:279-320 and source/src/App.tsx:357-419.

The production JavaScript and CSS outputs were 225.78 kB and 55.13 kB respectively, while the single 2,490.11 kB hero raster dominates the 2,771,498-byte production footprint and is the actionable performance concern.

categories.label: Verification readiness
evidence: source/package.json:6-10 provides separate typecheck and build scripts plus a combined check script, and source/tsconfig.json:2-17 enables strict type checking.
The supplied `npm run check` completed successfully with exit code 0, including TypeScript validation and a Vite production build.
Deterministic browser checks exercised desktop and mobile core paths and recorded zero uncaught console or page errors.

strengths: The complete destination-comparison, voting, winner, budget, travel-time, group-status, and itinerary experience is available without authentication or a backend.
Vote state is represented as one stable destination ID and all displayed totals and winner data are derived from it, avoiding counter drift.
The layout remained usable without page-level horizontal overflow at both 1440×900 and 390×844.
Strict type checking and the production build pass with the frozen dependency set.
No remote services, remote assets, external requests, or user-data collection are present.
risks: Screen-reader users encounter multiple destination vote controls with the same accessible name.
The sort select lacks a visible keyboard-focus treatment, and small comparison labels have insufficient contrast.
The 2.49 MB hero PNG dominates the production payload and is delivered unchanged to mobile devices.
recommendation: The core app is functionally complete and deployable. Before release, make vote controls destination-specific for assistive technology, restore a visible sort-control focus state, raise metric-label contrast, and ship responsive compressed variants of the hero image.
